deck.title: Onboarding Team Sync
deck.tagline: Weekly review · ship updates, experiment health, and what's next.

headlineFinding.kicker: Flow B is catching its target population
headlineFinding.explanation: matched a temporary-password account — invited barbers we want to catch before they hit Sign Up by mistake.
headlineFinding.takeaway: Without Flow B's email-first routing, this cohort would most likely have created accidental Indie accounts.

rollout.interpretation: First 6 days were at ≤20% rollout. Today is the first day at 50% — meaningful statistical reads start accumulating from tomorrow forward.

metrics.title: Variant balance — is the 50/50 split actually 50/50?
metrics.definition: Distinct devices (anonymous_id) assigned to each variant via LOGIN_EXPERIMENT_ASSIGNED, post-deploy.
metrics.whyItMatters: If the split is uneven, every downstream comparison is biased. We're working to understand the cause.
metrics.currentRead: Honestly, there's a deviation: 63/37 device split (418 vs 244) instead of 50/50 — confirmed in Snowflake (source of truth). LD shows 61/39, directionally consistent. Investigation in flight (Santi + Tristan).

metrics.eyebrow: Flow B insight · The money signal
metrics.title: Email check — who actually enters Flow B?
metrics.definition: EMAIL_CHECK_RESULT distribution by result type (returning · temp_password · new_user). Flow B exclusive.
metrics.whyItMatters: This is the routing decision Flow B was designed to extract. High temp_password share = catching invited barbers before they accidentally sign up.
metrics.currentRead: 33.5% temp_password — Flow B is catching invited barbers as designed. 43.7% returning, 14.1% new_user, ~9% legacy username inputs. The 33.5% slice represents the target population the experiment exists to capture.

metrics.title: Login completion funnel by variant
metrics.definition: Distinct devices at each step: assigned → welcome viewed → login succeeded. (Consistent ANONYMOUS_ID across all 3 steps — re-pull v3 done.)
metrics.whyItMatters: Tells us whether either flow is breaking login. Conversion rate = succeeded ÷ welcome.
metrics.currentRead: Flow B converts BETTER. Flow A: 418 → 358 → 210 (welcome 85.6%, conversion 58.7%). Flow B: 244 → 243 → 164 (welcome 99.6%, conversion 67.5%). Email-first doesn't break login — it improves it by ~9pp. Flow B's near-100% welcome rate confirms the design works as intended.

metrics.title: Friction signals — where users struggle
metrics.definition: Counts of LOGIN_ATTEMPT_FAILED, FORGOT_PASSWORD_TAPPED, and LOGIN_ABANDONED — per variant.
metrics.whyItMatters: Surfaces UX problems that completion rates alone miss.
metrics.currentRead: Flow B has 57 abandonment events at the NEW email_entry step (Flow A has no equivalent). Password-step abandonment: 202 events (Flow A) vs 123 (Flow B). Forgot password: 196 events (Flow A) vs 59 (Flow B). Login_failed: 14 events Flow B, 0 Flow A — likely older app versions emit the legacy event without the variant property; under investigation.

metrics.title: Accidental signups — Indie via signup_link
metrics.definition: ACCOUNT_CREATED where account_type = 'indie' AND came_from = 'signup_link', segmented by variant.
metrics.whyItMatters: The metric the experiment exists to move.
metrics.currentRead: Zero indie signups in either variant this window — likely an instrumentation gap (the indie account_type may not be emitting on creation), not a null result. Closest proxy: Flow A — 38 barber/signup_link signups (original problem path). Flow B — 34 barber/email_not_found signups (redirected path). QA validation pending.

perExposure.title: Per-exposure rates tell a different story
perExposure.subtitle: Counts the same kind of accidental barber signup, normalized per device. Flow B's rate is ~60% higher.

flowA.label: Flow A · accidental barber via signup_link
flowA.rateLabel: of Flow A devices created an accidental barber-type account
flowA.path: User sees Sign Up button → taps it → signs up by mistake.

flowB.label: Flow B · accidental barber via email_not_found
flowB.rateLabel: of Flow B devices created an accidental barber-type account
flowB.path: User types email → API returns not-found → auto-redirects to signup.

perExposure.honestTake: Counts ONLY accidental barber-type signups (not total signups). Not declaring a winner — volume small, 7 days at ramp <50% most of it. Worth understanding before the experiment matures.

caveats.title: ID-space mixing on funnel
caveats.detail: login_succeeded uses USER_ID; pre-login events use ANONYMOUS_ID. Funnel doesn't reconcile across steps until we use one ID throughout.
caveats.fix: Re-pull funnel with ANONYMOUS_ID across all steps — query in flight.

caveats.title: Indie north-star = 0 in window
caveats.detail: Both variants show zero ACCOUNT_CREATED with account_type='indie'. Likely an instrumentation gap: the indie property may not be emitted on creation. Volume too small to be a real null.
caveats.fix: QA-validate property emission for the indie account creation path.

caveats.title: Login_failed asymmetry · Flow A absent
caveats.detail: Flow A returns zero rows in LOGIN_FAILED with the variant filter. Likely older app versions emit the legacy event without the variant property; the renamed event ('Login attempt failed') hasn't fully ramped.
caveats.fix: Cross-check LOGIN_ATTEMPT_FAILED next pull, audit which app versions emit which event.

retrospective.context: The Onboarding squad is a small-team experiment within Squire — built to ship fast and iterate. This first project is the proof.

wentWell.title: Cross-platform parity at the wire level 🔁
wentWell.detail: iOS and Android emit identical event names + property values byte-for-byte. One Snowflake query covers both.

wentWell.title: Phased rollout managed risk 🛡️
wentWell.detail: 1% → 50% over 6 days, 100% tomorrow. No production incidents during the ramp.

wentWell.title: Analytics plan locked before code shipped 📋
wentWell.detail: Aligned with Tristan on north star + 9 events + threshold + caveats — guards against rationalizing post-hoc.

wentWell.title: Snowflake unblock → actionable data same day ⚡
wentWell.detail: Went from 'waiting for access' to 'first headline finding' within a single working session.

slowedDown.title: Unknowns surfaced during QA, not before 🧪
slowedDown.detail: Khem (QA) flagged that login needed to accept usernames. iOS auto sign-in flow wasn't tested → late fix.
slowedDown.learning: Pre-QA spike to enumerate edge cases (legacy user types, auto-flows) before implementation locks in.

slowedDown.title: Concurrent feature-flag tooling migration 🔀
slowedDown.detail: LD → PostHog → GrowthBook in motion at the same time. Wiring overhead + source-of-truth confusion.
slowedDown.learning: Lock the analytics destination before instrumenting; treat tooling migration as a separate workstream.

slowedDown.title: Cross-platform context switching on unfamiliar code 📱
slowedDown.detail: iOS + Android were both ramp-up territory. Ship work doubles when you're not the expert on either.
slowedDown.learning: Mitigation in place — future onboarding work moves to React Native (familiar territory).

slowedDown.title: Side project (Weekly Digest) — scope clarified 📊
slowedDown.detail: Bugs from Reporting team were eating dev time on this experiment.
slowedDown.learning: Resolved: 1 day/week allocated to Digest; new dev from Artur's team handles going forward.

slowedDown.title: Access provisioning not pre-staged 🔑
slowedDown.detail: Snowflake + flag service accesses surfaced as bottlenecks AFTER ship, delaying the first analytics pull by days.
slowedDown.learning: File access requests on day 1 of project, not when needed.

commitmentDecisions.text: Coordinate with RN launch (29 May) — full feature parity, or onboarding-only first?
commitmentDecisions.ask: Confirm with Eric + Yurii in this meeting

commitmentDecisions.text: Phased Courses prototyping — stages with Tristan, or one big push?
Build login flows (A and B) in the new RN app in parallel with Appointments team

commitmentFyi: Re-pull data with consistent ANONYMOUS_ID across all funnel steps (query drafted)
Investigate root cause of SRM with Tristan — Snowflake confirms 64/36
Audit signup_link_tapped scope leak (3 stray Flow B events) + login_failed asymmetry
iOS + Android stability monitoring during 100% rollout (push Android Title Case rename once stable)
Identify privacy team owner for user_id property sign-off
Continue weekly analytics extraction — this deck, fresh data each week

courses.title: Courses · indie onboarding next chapter
courses.context: Indie onboarding restructured around course-based education. Already kicked off pre-experiment — paused during Tristan's vacation. Resuming now.
